evidence_source: R/family.R:113-124 (skew_normal() family, mu link=identity); src/drmTMB.cpp:2424-2463 (model_type==17 likelihood, mu=X_mu*beta_mu, native xi/omega/alpha transform); R/drmTMB.R:3557-3773 (drm_build_skew_normal_ls_spec); tests/testthat/test-skew-normal-location-scale.R:72-101,128-169,171-199,201-230,252-280 (deterministic single/quasi-seed point recovery across plain, factor, correlated-predictor, and slope DGPs, |coef-truth| bounded 0.08-0.60); docs/dev-log/skew-normal-pilot.md:12-53 (formal 200-rep/cell ADEMP pilot, mu bias<=0.012 and RMSE<=0.10 at n=100, <=0.006/0.05 at n=400); docs/dev-log/simulation-artifacts/2026-06-17-skew-normal-fixed-effect-pilot/tables/skew-normal-fe-wald-coverage.csv (mu Wald-CI rows return finite coverage 0.84-1.00 at nominal 0.70, 25 reps/cell)
notes: Fixed-effect location formula is required (exactly one mu entry, R/drmTMB.R:3601-3605). Wald confint()/profile_targets() return finite mu intervals via the generic per-dpar mechanism (directly test-confirmed for the sibling nu dpar at test-skew-normal-location-scale.R:282-357; same code path applies to mu). No formal 500-/1000-replicate coverage/MCSE campaign exists for mu (docs/dev-log/known-limitations.md:324-333: "not a formal 500- or 1000-replicate operating characteristics result"); the 2026-06-17 pilot README explicitly disclaims "calibrated interval language." meta_V() known-covariance is separately rejected for this family (R/drmTMB.R:3649-3655). REML is unavailable regardless of dpar (see REML row below).

evidence_source: R/drmTMB.R:1978-1986 (drm_validate_reml_spec: cli_abort "REML is implemented only for univariate and bivariate Gaussian models" unless model_type is gaussian or biv_gaussian)
notes: Generic non-Gaussian REML gate fires before any dpar/effect-type/formula check, so it blocks REML unconditionally for every skew_normal row in this census (not re-derived per row below).

evidence_source: R/family.R:113-124 (log link); src/drmTMB.cpp:2426,2428-2432 (log_sigma=X_sigma*beta_sigma, optional soft-clamp, sigma=exp(log_sigma)); R/drmTMB.R:3615-3619 (defaults to sigma~1 when omitted); tests/testthat/test-skew-normal-location-scale.R:72-101,171-199,252-280 (deterministic recovery, |coef(sigma)-truth|<=0.08-0.20); docs/dev-log/skew-normal-pilot.md:12-53 (200-rep/cell pilot, sigma:(Intercept) bias<=0.021 at n=100, <=0.004 at n=400, truth on log scale = 0); docs/dev-log/simulation-artifacts/2026-06-17-skew-normal-fixed-effect-pilot/tables/skew-normal-fe-wald-coverage.csv (sigma Wald-CI rows, coverage 0.84-1.00 at nominal 0.70, 25 reps/cell)
notes: Public sigma is the response SD (R/family.R:90-99: "stats::sigma() returns SD[y] = sigma"), unlike student's native scale parameter. Same evidentiary basis as mu-fixed: point recovery plus mechanically finite (uncalibrated) Wald CIs; no formal coverage campaign. REML unavailable regardless (see REML row below).
Same generic non-Gaussian REML gate as the mu-fixed row; not dpar-specific.

evidence_source: src/drmTMB.cpp:2424-2463 (nu=eta_nu identity link; native alpha=nu, delta=alpha/sqrt(1+alpha^2) skew transform at lines 2439-2445); R/drmTMB.R:3596-3599,3620-3624,3643-3647 (nu formula must be one-sided fixed-effect residual slant; defaults to nu~1); tests/testthat/test-skew-normal-location-scale.R:128-169,201-230,282-357 (deterministic slant-sign/magnitude recovery, direction recovery, and profile_targets()/confint() mechanics for nu:x); R/profile.R:1710-1727 (skew_normal_slant_targets()/warn_skew_normal_slant_wald(): cli_warn "Wald confidence interval(s) for the skew-normal slant ... miscalibrated near nu = 0, where the Azzalini information is near-singular"); tests/testthat/test-confint-skew-normal-slant.R:19-41 (confint(fit) and confint(fit, parm="fixef:nu:(Intercept)") both warn by default; method="profile" does not warn); docs/dev-log/skew-normal-pilot.md:55-66 (formal 200-rep/cell 95% Wald slant-CI coverage: 59.5%-76.0% at true nu=0 across n in {100,400}, MCSE~3%, false-positive-at-nu0 24.0%-40.5%; coverage recovers to 91.7%-95.0% at true nu=4)
notes: The ONLY dpar with a documented, in-code-flagged interval-calibration failure: the package's own confint() warns and redirects to profile/bootstrap, and the formal ADEMP pilot quantifies severe Wald undercoverage (up to 40.5% false-positive rate) near nu=0. Point estimates still recover (deterministic tests pass) so this is not "not_implemented," but the interval evidence is a documented problem, not a feasibility demonstration -- hence diagnostic_only rather than interval_feasible. Profile/bootstrap nu intervals do not warn (test-confint-skew-normal-slant.R:35-41) but have no coverage campaign either: the 2026-06-17 pilot README explicitly excludes "profile/bootstrap interval calibration" from its scope, so profile/bootstrap should be read as unvalidated-but-recommended, not as a calibrated fix. REML unavailable regardless (see REML row below).
Same generic non-Gaussian REML gate; moot in practice since nu also carries its own interval-calibration caveat under ML (see nu-fixed ML row).

evidence_source: R/methods.R:5035-5066 (drm_dpar_link switch has no biv_skew_normal/skew_normal2 case); R/drmTMB.R:2590-2607 (drm_composed_families dispatch: only c(gaussian(), gaussian())/list(gaussian(), gaussian()) routes to biv_gaussian; any other same- or mixed-family pair, including c(skew_normal(), skew_normal()), hits "Mixed-response bivariate families are not implemented yet."); R/family.R:103-106 (roxygen: "bivariate skew-normal models, residual rho12 ... remain planned but unsupported in this first slice")
notes: Representative row for the whole bivariate axis (mu shown; sigma/nu identical): no biv_skew_normal model_type exists anywhere in src/drmTMB.cpp or R/methods.R, and the family-pair dispatcher does not special-case two skew_normal() responses -- skew_normal is univariate-only end to end.
Applies to "(1 | id)"-style intercept terms. Estimator ML shown as representative; REML is separately and unconditionally rejected for skew_normal regardless of RE status (see mu-fixed REML row).

Applies to "(0 + x | id)"-style independent-slope terms; validate_skew_normal_random_terms only checks length(terms) > 0 and does not distinguish term$type, so slopes are rejected by the identical gate as intercepts (no dedicated slope test exists, but the same unconditional check governs both). Estimator ML shown as representative; REML independently rejected too.

notes: Applies to "(1 | id)"-style intercept terms on sigma. Estimator ML shown as representative; REML independently rejected too.
Same unconditional length(terms) > 0 gate as the sigma-intercept row; no term$type distinction exists. Estimator ML shown as representative; REML independently rejected too.

evidence_source: R/drmTMB.R:3664-3666 (extract_random_mu_terms(nu_entry$rhs, "nu") reused for the nu formula, then validate_skew_normal_random_terms(nu_re$terms, "nu")); R/drmTMB.R:7742-7753; tests/testthat/test-skew-normal-location-scale.R:433-439 (bf(y ~ x, sigma ~ z, nu ~ x + (1 | id)) errors "random effects are not implemented")
notes: Applies to "(1 | id)"-style intercept terms on nu. Unlike student/tweedie, skew_normal nu random effects hit the family-specific validator (not the generic nu/tau shape-RE branch in drm_reject_phase1_terms), because the RE term is stripped by extract_random_mu_terms before drm_reject_phase1_terms ever runs. Estimator ML shown as representative; REML independently rejected too.
Same unconditional length(terms) > 0 gate as the nu-intercept row; no term$type distinction exists. Estimator ML shown as representative; REML independently rejected too.
